title: EoTV was Air confirmed in December Newtype
display_title: EoTV was Air confirmed in December Newtype
wikitext:
[]: EoTV was Air, confirmed in December Newtype?             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Sep 18, 2011 10:44 am Bochan_bird http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2003-November/000661.html : There was a special Newtype magazine issue (December extra issue) devoted entirely to Evangelion, which also contained a DVD with samples of Eva2 game scenarios. In addition to spotlighting Eva2, this magazine also contained a 1-page summary of each TV episode with "checkpoints" which are like the Newtype TV filmbook checkpoints but with even less information. IMO the only thing worth noting was the Ep25/26 checkpoints which stated quite clearly that EoE (mainly "Air") was the originally intended TV ending, but could not be made due to production schedule and other reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sun Sep 18, 2011 11:07 am Ehmm, wasn't EoE made from the original screenplay drafts of episodes 25 and 26? They (I don't know who exactly) don't wanted those episodes to be on TV since they were to violent, also the production problems (I thought they were going to be really expensive) forced Hideaki Anno to make EoTV as we know it and give up on EoE. Then I don't know what helped Anno to make EoE (Enraged fans? Those women in EoE credits?) These are the rumors I've heard for more than a decade. Sorry for any (or all) the misinformation.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Sep 18, 2011 11:17 am John wrote: Ehmm, wasn't EoE made from the original screenplay drafts of episodes 25 and 26?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Sep 18, 2011 11:25 am Anno has always maintained that the televised ending was the one he had planned for originally. That said, I've never believed him.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sun Sep 18, 2011 11:50 am Trajan wrote: Anno has always maintained that the televised ending was the one he had planned for originally. That said, I've never believed him.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Sep 18, 2011 12:08 pm John wrote: Anno has that interesting ability to change facts/opinions/ideas over time

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:36 pm We've already had official confirmation for this in the RCB: Episode 25' "Air" is based on the original episode 25 script which was completed during production of the TV series. Due to production time limits and other problems, this script was not used and the TV episode 25 "Owaru sekai (The Ending World)" instead became a drama which unfolded within an inner universe like episode 26. In this sense, episode 25' could be considered a return to the originally intended contents. In contrast, episode 26' adds much more story and dramatic content to TV episode 26, thus deepening the theme.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:43 pm NAveryW wrote: We've already had official confirmation for this in the RCB:

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 19, 2011 9:24 pm Trajan wrote: Anno has always maintained that the televised ending was the one he had planned for originally. That said, I've never believed him.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 24, 2011 8:21 am Also, that EoE was planned first doesn't necessaraly vindicate concurrency, or imply that EoTv is somehow "watered down" or a lesser placeholder. A few sources state that they simply weren't thst sure how to end it...
